text:
Once upon a time, in a cozy little town nestled between mountains and flowers, there lived a wise old owl named Harold. Harold was renowned far and wide for his insightful wisdom and his peculiar interest in human tales. One sunny afternoon, while scanning the villagers’ logs for news, he heard a story about a brave little hedgehog who saved a town from a giant mouse.

The mouse, Glenda the Giantess, claimed she was just rescuing garden critters. However, her penchant for havoc soon became well-known. One rain-soaked day, noticing little hedgehogs drowning, Glenda decided it was time for an intervention.

Harold couldn’t resist. He hooted cheerfully, his feathered cloak billowing gently. "Oh dear! You’ll have to go consult Merlin the owl."

So off Glenda went, with Harold fluttering after her like a little bat. Finally, upon Merlin's wise advice, Glenda returned home. She apologized profusely and gave
